latest.title: Notice of Results and Investor Meeting
latest.content:
28 Apr 2026 10:15
RNS Number : 2078C
GETECH Group plc
28 April 2026
28th April 2026
Getech Group plc
("Getech" or the "Company")
Notice of Results and Investor Presentation
Getech (AIM: GTC), a world-leading locator of subsurface resources, is pleased to confirm that it will announce its Final Results for the 12 months to 31 December 2025 on Thursday 30 April 2026.
CEO Chris Jepps and CFO Simon Brown will provide a live presentation relating to the Final Results via Investor Meet Company on 30 Apr 2026 at 14:30.
Investors can sign up to Investor Meet Company and add to meet GETECH GROUP PLC via:
https://www.investormeetcompany.com/getech-group-plc/register-investor
For further information, please contact:
Getech Group plc
Chris Jepps, CEO
Tel: 0113 322 2200
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited
Neil McDonald / Pete Lynch (Corporate Finance)
Jasper Berry / Dale Bellis (Sales)
Tel: 020 7908 6000
Novella Communications
Tel: 020 3151 7008
Tim Robertson
getech@novella-comms.com
Notes to editors:
About Getech
Getech provides trusted subsurface and geospatial insight to organisations across the global natural resources sector. We help corporates, governments, and regulators find and manage the subsurface resources essential to the evolving energy and minerals landscape by combining unique global earth‑science data, leading geospatial expertise and advanced analytics. Our solutions reduce risk, accelerate opportunities and enhance project value throughout the resource lifecycle, supporting activity across oil and gas, critical minerals, geothermal, natural hydrogen and other emerging resource domains.
Founded in 1994 Getech is listed on the Alternative Investment Market of the London Stock Exchange ("AIM"), with ticker symbol GTC.
For further information, please visit
www.getech.com
.

latest.title: Major Globe Platform Subscription Renewal
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 6672Y
GETECH Group plc
31 March 2026
31st March 2026
Getech Group plc
("Getech" or the "Company")
Major Globe Platform Subscription Renewal
Multi-year extension to Getech's subsurface intelligence platform
Getech (AIM: GTC), a world-leading locator of subsurface resources, is pleased to announce that it has successfully closed a significant Globe platform subscription renewal with a major state-backed Asian offshore oil and gas producer. The renewal contract has a total value of c. £520k to be recognised over three years.
Globe enhances resource exploration activities by providing a 'digital twin' - or computer model - of Earth's geologic development. Its users leverage this unique model to help locate potential zones for new resource discovery.
Completion of this renewal underscores continued demand for Getech's subsurface intelligence solutions and the value Globe provides to customers' exploration programmes.
Chris Jepps, CEO of Getech, commented:
"We are pleased that a key Globe customer has renewed its subscription for a further three years, extending its engagement with the platform to over six years, reflecting the customer's continued investment in exploration as part of the future global energy mix, and reinforcing the relevance of our products and services to this sector.
The contract adds to our order book and strengthens Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR), with a 28% renewal value uplift reflecting inflationary adjustments and enhanced product capability, in line with our ARR growth strategy."
For further information, please contact:
Getech Group plc
Chris Jepps, CEO
Tel: 0113 322 2200
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited
Neil McDonald / Pete Lynch (Corporate Finance)
Jasper Berry / Dale Bellis (Sales)
Tel: 020 7908 6000

Tel: 020 3151 7008
Tim Robertson / Aeliya Bilgrami
getech@novella-comms.com

Our solutions reduce risk, accelerate opportunities and enhance project value throughout the resource lifecycle, supporting activity across oil and gas, critical minerals, geothermal, natural hydrogen and other emerging resource domains.

latest.title: Trading Statement
latest.content:
20 Jan 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4980P
GETECH Group plc
20 January 2026
20th January 2026
Getech Group plc
("Getech" or the "Company")
Trading Update
Return to Growth with EBITDA Performance Ahead of Market Expectations
Getech (AIM: GTC), a world-leading locator of subsurface resources, is pleased to provide the following trading update (unaudited) for the financial year ended 31 December 2025.
2025 Highlights:
·
Turnaround plan successfully delivered by new management team over the last 11 months.
·
Resulting in improved trading with the Company expecting 6% revenue growth to £5.0m (2024: £4.7m), and adjusted EBITDA of £0.5m (2024: loss of £0.6m) - the first positive EBITDA figure for the Company since 2019.
·
Introduced new sustainable business strategy - refocusing the Group on its traditional markets of Oil & Gas and Mining whilst also maintaining a focus on selective Natural Hydrogen projects.
·
Re-set the Company's financial base, reducing costs by c. 20% - a c. £1.0m annualised reduction.
·
Expanded and re-organised global sales team under new sales Director, leading to improved size and quality of new business pipeline.
·
The Group orderbook stood at £3.8m at 31 December 2025 (2024: £4.1m), reflecting the natural conversion of contracted orderbook into revenue.
·
Cash at bank of £0.2m at 31 December 2025, held back by a delayed £0.4m payment caused by the US government shutdown in Q4 FY25 which was received after the period end. Receivables at 31 December 2025 totalled £1.5m, with debt of £0.15m.
·
Annual recurring revenue ("ARR") at end FY25 was £2.8m (2024: £2.9m), expected to improve in 2026 backed by a pipeline of prospective clients looking to utilise the Globe platform under multi-year contracts.
2026 Outlook:
·
In 2025 Getech established itself as a market leader in the nascent but potentially vast Natural Hydrogen market via selective project identification and anticipates further focused activity in the current year.
·
Positive momentum has carried into 2026, supported by a deeper sales pipeline that offers greater visibility of increased future revenues and activity.
Chris Jepps, CEO of Getech, said:
"We have achieved the goals we set at the start of the year. We did this by resetting the Company cost base without restricting our ability to deliver projects and grow revenues, and by taking the business back to its roots by focusing on the markets we know best: Oil & Gas, Mining and Natural Hydrogen.
The trading performance has been very satisfying. Our objective now is to build momentum by continuing to grow our revenue lines and in particular focusing on materially adding to ARR via our subscription products, both with new clients and by expanding existing contracts.
Ultimately, we are looking to achieve a better balance across the business with a higher level of retained, predictable income that better covers the base costs of the business while allowing us to successfully close the harder to forecast material deals which will drive out-performance.
As a new management team we have taken some important strides in the last year and I believe we now have a solid platform to build upon."
This announcement contains inside information as stipulated under the UK version of the Market Abuse Regulation No 596/2014 which is part of English Law by virtue of the European (Withdrawal) Act 2018, as amended. On publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this information is considered to be in the public domain.
For further information, please contact:
Getech Group plc
Chris Jepps, CEO
Tel: 0113 322 2200
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited
Neil McDonald / Pete Lynch (Corporate Finance)
Jasper Berry / Dale Bellis (Sales)
Tel: 020 7908 6000
Novella Communications
Tel: 020 3151 7008
Tim Robertson / Aeliya Bilgrami
getech@novella-comms.com
Notes to editors:
About Getech
Getech is a leading locator of subsurface resources essential for the world's energy security and sustainable transition. The Company combines unique geoscience expertise, AI-driven analytics, and extensive geospatial insight to identify new energy and mineral resources and streamline exploration processes. Serving clients across corporates, government and regulators in a wide range of industries, Getech is committed to enabling energy and mineral security while supporting a shift towards low carbon sources.
Founded in 1994 Getech is listed on the Alternative Investment Market of the London Stock Exchange ("AIM"), with ticker symbol GTC.
For further information, please visit
www.getech.com
.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: GTC provides cloud‑based data‑analytics software that helps small and medium businesses turn raw data into actionable insights. The platform integrates AI‑driven forecasting, reporting and workflow automation, enabling clients to improve operational efficiency without building in‑house solutions.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global market for business‑intelligence and analytics software is growing fast, driven by the need for real‑time data and AI capabilities. GTC’s low‑cost, plug‑and‑play offering gives it an edge over larger, more complex vendors, positioning the company to capture a share of the expanding SMB analytics space.
company_30s.current_state: GTC is in a growth‑phase transition: it has recently secured multiple contract wins, entered a joint venture in the hydrogen sector and renewed its core platform subscription base. The company is still in the early revenue‑generation stage, with cash burn being managed through new deals and share‑option grants.

rns_narrative: GTC has been busy renewing key platform subscriptions and winning new contracts, which signals that its core product is gaining traction.
A strategic pivot is underway with the formation of a hydrogen joint venture, suggesting the company is looking to diversify revenue streams.
Corporate governance changes – new directors and shareholding updates – indicate a reshuffle aimed at strengthening the board and aligning incentives.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet skepticism – investors are listening but not buying.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders note the hydrogen JV as a game‑changer and see the contract wins as proof of product demand. They argue the company is on a path to profitability if the new ventures materialise.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others point to the recent price drop after the RNS and the fact that the company still struggles to hit revenue targets. They fear the JV may not deliver revenue quickly enough to offset the current losses.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has shifted from neutral to cautious optimism after the JV announcement, but the overall volume of posts remains low, keeping the market largely indifferent.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for GTC is flat – no public interest spikes, which gives the contrarian a clean slate for surprise.

near_term_catalysts: Earnings release in Q3 – a surprise in revenue or gross margin could trigger a bounce.
Milestone in the hydrogen JV – first signed order or partnership agreement would validate the diversification strategy.
Renewal of a large platform contract – a multi‑year commitment would signal customer confidence.
Volume spike around support levels – a sudden influx of buyers could break the low and start the recovery.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained volume increase of 30%+ over the previous week, the price breaking the recent low by at least 10%, and a positive earnings surprise of 10%+ over expectations.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is deep in a crash‑recovery cycle. The APEX score indicates tight compression, suggesting a mean‑reversion impulse is likely to come once volume starts to pick up. The current price sits at a low that could be a natural support for a rebound.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The price is more than 70% below its recent high – a textbook deep crash. If the company’s new contracts and the hydrogen JV materialise, the stock could climb back to 50% of its pre‑crash level, giving a 50% upside. Historically, such deep bottoms have produced 2× to 3× returns once the catalyst fires.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Revenue growth is still modest and the hydrogen JV is unproven. If the company fails to secure a sizable order from the JV or the earnings miss by a wide margin, the price could stay at the low for an extended period, risking a 20‑30% further decline.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 50% if the price recovers to the midway point. Potential downside: 20% if the price stays flat or falls a bit further. The reward exceeds the risk by roughly 2.5:1, making the position attractive for a contrarian long.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter once the price closes above the recent low and volume is at least 30% higher than the 7‑day average. Use a 10% stop‑loss below the low. Size the position at 5–7% of the portfolio, scaling in if the price continues to climb.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the Q3 earnings or the JV milestone announcement. If the earnings beat expectations or a JV order is confirmed, consider a small position to test the market.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks identified; the deep crash itself is a sufficient catalyst for a rebound. No need to exit early unless the company shows signs of bankruptcy or regulatory issues.

ai_insights.bottom_line: GTC’s price is in a very deep crash, and the recent hydrogen joint venture plus new contracts could spark a mean‑reversion bounce, offering a 2–3× upside with a modest downside – a classic contrarian play.
